items.title: Immunoproteasome remodeling in senescing human macrophages reveals the loss of PA28αβ capping as a hallmark of immunosenescence
items.author: Rita Crinelli
items.content: A study on proteasome complex dynamics in an in vitro model of senescent human macrophages and in the lymph nodes of aged mice reveals immunoproteasome remodeling and the loss of PA28αβ regulator capping as hallmarks of immunosenescence.

items.title: Genetic mapping of complement system proteins for islet autoimmunity in children with high risk of T1D
items.author: Ani Manichaikul
items.content: Protein quantitative trait locus mapping of complement proteins sheds light on the underlying genetic factors from the complement system and their role in the etiology of islet autoimmunity and T1D for young children at high risk of developing T1D.

items.title: Archaic adaptive introgression in modern human reproductive genes
items.author: Bence Viola
items.content: Modern human reproductive genes show evidence of adaptive introgression due to gene flow from Neanderthals and Denisovans in the past.

items.title: IL6ST participates in the development of adolescent idiopathic scoliosis by regulating bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells
items.author: Hong-Qi Zhang
items.content: IL6ST participates in the development of adolescent idiopathic scoliosis by regulating the osteogenic differentiation of bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells.

items.title: Divergent community assembly processes and multifunctionality contributions of abundant and rare soil bacteria during a 53-year restoration in the Tengger Desert, China
items.author: Jianming Deng
items.content: Long-term restoration of desertified ecosystems reveals distinct assembly processes and multifunctionality contributions of abundant and rare soil bacteria.

items.title: Arabidopsis MYB47 and MYB95 transcription factors regulate jasmonate-inducible ER-body formation
items.author: Kenji Yamada
items.content: In response to wounding and jasmonate treatment, Arabidopsis thaliana transcription factors MYB47 and MYB95 control the formation of ER bodies, which are a component of a mechanism defending plants against insects and fungi.

items.title: Brain insulin receptor gene network shapes risk for metabolic disease after early-life stress in women
items.author: Patricia Pelufo Silveira
items.content: How the brain responds to early-life stress may shape future health: individual differences in the expression of an insulin receptor gene network in the prefrontal cortex and striatum influence the risk of visceral fat accumulation and metabolic syndrome, especially in women exposed to early-life stress.

items.title: Multi-omics data reveal the origin of cardiac myxoma
items.author: Xiaoping Li
items.content: The effect of zingerone on postmenopausal osteoporosis, as modelled in mouse, was assessed The authors combine single-cell transcriptomics and bulk metabolomics to investigate cardiac myxoma. They identify Subtype 1 marked by MAPK/WNT/EGFR pathway activation and Subtype 2, characterized by ribosomal and oxidative phosphorylation signatures
